retrieved_docs.title: Nystagmus Types -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Post-rotational nystagmus: reflexive horizontal nystagmus that occurs with a rapid brake to a unidirectional head rotation, with a contralateral fast phase and a subjective rocking boat sensation

retrieved_docs.title: Lensmaker’s Equation -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The lensmaker equation is a mathematical computation to determine the focal length of a lens in air. The focal length of a lens is defined as the distance from the center of the lens to an optical point to which all light rays are refracted. [1]

retrieved_docs.title: Nystagmus Types -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Per-rotational nystagmus: horizontal jerk nystagmus that occurs with sustained head rotations, with fast phases ipsilateral to the rotation

retrieved_docs.title: Sonography Physical Principles And Instrumentation -- Definition/Introduction -- Basic Ultrasound Physics
retrieved_docs.content: Like a rock being dropped into a still pond with the waves bouncing off the shore, the sound waves emitted by the transducer resonate and refract back to the transducer. When the ultrasound reverberates off tissue and returns, it induces vibration into the PZT crystals, which are converted back into an electric signal. The processing computer within the ultrasound machine then generates the signals into 2D (2 dimensional) and, in more recent times, 3D (3 dimensional) images. The frequency (and, inversely, wavelength) of the sound waves produced can be modified to produce greater penetration through soft tissue by modifying the power or gain. The lower the frequency, the deeper or greater the sound wave penetrance. Unfortunately, the more penetrance is obtained, the poorer the image quality. Lastly, some common terminology unique to the ultrasound field be discussed. Hyperechoic is defined by an object that is brighter than the surrounding tissue. Hypoechoic or anechoic is the opposite, by which an object is darker. Both of which are determined by the density of the object.

retrieved_docs.title: Modified Ashworth Scale -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The assessment begins with the joint in either a fully flexed or fully extended position, depending on the muscle group being tested. The examiner should passively move the limb through its full range of motion at a controlled speed, ideally completing the movement within approximately 1 second. The movement should be performed smoothly and without excessive force.

retrieved_docs.title: Motor Vehicle Collisions -- Definition/Introduction -- Mechanism of Injury
retrieved_docs.content: According to the Law of Conservation of Energy, “Energy can neither be destroyed or created, but it can change form.” This law goes to the essence of trauma. The kinetic or moving energy must be transferred onto the object impacted. So, if a vehicle is traveling at a high rate of speed when it impacts an object, the energy force can be quadrupled. Spinal injuries may be a result of axial loads. [4] [5] [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Trendelenburg Sign -- Definition/Introduction -- Prerequisites of Testing
retrieved_docs.content: The patient can stand on the affected side for more than 30 seconds The patient can understand commands The patient has intact coordination The patient has free abduction movement; no fixed adduction deformity

retrieved_docs.title: Beevor Sign -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The Beevor sign is characterized by abnormal cephalad movement of the umbilicus during truncal flexion while a patient is supine. [1] In individuals with normal muscle function, the rectus abdominis contracts as a single unit without a predominance of the upper over the lower portion or the left over the right side. [2] Consequently, the navel remains stationary during trunk flexion.

retrieved_docs.title: Nystagmus Types -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Physiologic end-point nystagmus: horizontal jerks nystagmus results from testing oculomotor movements too far laterally

retrieved_docs.title: Sonography Physical Principles And Instrumentation -- Definition/Introduction -- Basic Ultrasound Physics
retrieved_docs.content: As the name implies, ultrasound uses sound waves at a higher frequency than audible for the human ear. For example, the average human ear hears frequencies from the 20 hertz to 20 kHz (kilohertz) range, whereas the typical clinical ultrasound uses frequencies from 1 to 20 MHz (megahertz). A simple understanding of sinusoidal waves is useful for understanding ultrasound better. Wavelength ( λ ) is inversely proportional to frequency ( f ) and proportionate to the velocity ( v ), as depicted in the following equation. λ =v /f

retrieved_docs.title: Lensmaker’s Equation -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The lensmaker equation quantifies the relationship between the focal length, refractive index, thickness, and curvature of a lens and is used to produce a lens with a desired focal point; this is the ideal lens for a given refraction. [2]

retrieved_docs.title: Modified Ashworth Scale -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: 0: No increase in muscle tone 1: Slight increase in muscle tone, with a catch and release or minimal resistance at the end of the range of motion when an affected part(s) is moved in flexion or extension 1+: Slight increase in muscle tone, manifested as a catch, followed by minimal resistance through the remainder (less than half) of the range of motion 2: A marked increase in muscle tone throughout most of the range of motion, but affected part(s) are still easily moved 3: Considerable increase in muscle tone, passive movement difficult 4: Affected part(s) rigid in flexion or extension

retrieved_docs.title: CT Instrumentation and Physics -- Definition/Introduction -- Summary and additional points to consider
retrieved_docs.content: Pitch is the ratio of the distance the table moved in a single x-ray tube rotation divided by the beam width. A beam width of 1 will have no slice overlap. A pitch greater than 1 will have slice gaps, while a less than 1 will overlap. [22]

retrieved_docs.title: Westphal Sign -- Definition/Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Westphal’s sign refers to a decreased or absent patellar tendon reflex, also known as the knee-jerk reflex, observed on physical exam. The patellar tendon reflex refers to a kicking-like motion produced by the extension of the knee joint upon the ipsilateral stimulation of the patellar tendon. The reflex is thought to play a role in maintaining balance. The realization of the importance of deep tendon reflexes in the physical exam is often attributed to Wilhelm Heinrich Erb and Carl Friedrich Otto Westphal, who both noted this finding in the late 1800s. [1] [2] Westphal’s sign thus receives its name from Carl Friedrich Otto Westphal. The patellar tendon reflex is a deep tendon reflex, similar to the biceps, brachioradialis, triceps, Achilles, and jaw jerk reflexes. [3]

retrieved_docs.content: In the European Union, the European Accessibility Act is equivalent to the ADA. Other regions have enacted requirements that address similar issues (see Table 1. Examples of Governmental Requirements Enacted in Various Nations).

retrieved_docs.title: Motor Vehicle Collisions -- Definition/Introduction -- Mechanism of Injury
retrieved_docs.content: Energy transfer from the environment can result from a variety of causes. The intensity of the energy transfer will cause damage to the surrounding tissues, organs, muscles, and other body structures when it exceeds the body’s ability to resist such rapid, forceful energy changes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 4 -- Community 2817
retrieved_docs.content: Výchova is a concept that may be qualified by specific medical procedures and imaging modalities. Both gastrostomy—whether performed as a temporary or permanent intervention—and spinal fusion are identified as allowed qualifiers for výchova. In addition, ultrazvuk (ultrasound) can be used to qualify výchova, indicating that imaging findings may also be relevant when applying this term in a clinical or documentation context.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 2 -- Community 1057
retrieved_docs.content: grouped under a unified late‑effect classification that is further subdivided by anatomical region. This structured relationship aids clinicians in linking acute injury diagnoses with their potential long‑term sequelae and facilitates comprehensive patient management across different injury types and body regions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2666
retrieved_docs.content: In medical terminology, a complication can be associated with the presence of multiple open wounds that themselves are not complicated, indicating that a broader clinical complication may arise even when each individual wound does not exhibit additional problems.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2623
retrieved_docs.content: The phrase “Due to” is associated with crushing injury or internal injury, and crushing injury or internal injury is in turn associated with the Arabic term “إصابة,” which means injury; thus, when a condition is described as “due to” a crushing or internal injury, it denotes a direct causal link to an injury.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2430
retrieved_docs.content: Other miscellaneous procedures are classified as a subset of other nonoperative procedures, indicating that they fall under the broader category of nonoperative interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 784
retrieved_docs.content: Complication is linked to multiple open wounds that have not yet resulted in additional problems, and it is also associated with open wounds of the extremities. This relationship underscores that even when open wounds—particularly numerous ones or those located on the limbs—have not produced further complications, they are still considered relevant to the broader concept of complication in clinical assessment and patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2804
retrieved_docs.content: Ultrazvuk, which denotes ultrasound, is directly linked with المعالجة بموجات فوق صوتية, the Arabic term for ultrasound wave therapy, indicating that ultrasound technology is employed as a therapeutic modality in medical treatment.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 5 -- Community 2903
retrieved_docs.content: Allowed qualifier is linked to several etiological concepts. It is associated with the etiology of ولادة قبل الأوان (premature birth) and also with the etiology of التموت (death). In addition, acquired foot deformities are identified as an etiological factor related to the allowed qualifier.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 1 -- Community 61

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2578
retrieved_docs.content: Acute and unspecified renal failure is described as being related to and possibly synonymous with كلاء الكليون السفلي, indicating that the two terms may refer to the same clinical concept.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2346
retrieved_docs.content: Prolapsed arm of fetus, unspecified as to episode of care or not applicable is classified as a specific instance of تدلّي ذراع الجنين, indicating that this fetal arm prolapse condition falls under the broader clinical concept of تدلّي ذراع الجنين.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 4 -- Community 2908
retrieved_docs.content: Other and ill-defined heart disease is synonymous with تمدد الأوعية الدموية في القلب, a term that describes dilation of the blood vessels within the heart. This equivalence indicates that the ambiguous classification of certain cardiac conditions corresponds to a pathological enlargement of the coronary or myocardial vasculature, which can affect cardiac function and warrants appropriate diagnostic evaluation and management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 4 -- Community 2902
retrieved_docs.content: The concept “Without” is associated with قيلة مستقيمية.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2343
retrieved_docs.content: Malposition and malpresentation of fetus is categorized as a specific condition within the broader domain of normal delivery and other indications for care in pregnancy, labor, and delivery, indicating that it is considered a child concept of this comprehensive obstetric care category. This relationship highlights that malposition and malpresentation are recognized as particular scenarios that fall under the general guidelines and considerations for managing normal deliveries and related clinical indications during pregnancy and childbirth.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 12 -- Level 3 -- Community 2031
retrieved_docs.content: Glaucoma includes the SMQ entity الزَرَق, and the entity الزَرَق is classified as a member of the broader group بضع الزاوية, indicating that the specific concept الزَرَق falls within the larger category بضع الزاوية while being a component of glaucoma.
